principles: No infinite loops: every dispute converges to a decision within a bounded number of attempts.
dev and QA cannot change requirements — only the arbiter (Architect / Design Authority) or the Product Owner can.
Escalate UP when a tier's cap is exceeded; never loop sideways.

ladder.note: Normal fix<->reopen. QA must cite the failing gate + evidence on every reopen; flaky tests are quarantined, not reopened.

resolutions.qa_too_strict: adjust gate threshold/criteria (QA + Architect) then re-verify
resolutions.requirement_ambiguous: amend design doc / game data to disambiguate; reset acceptance; re-dispatch
resolutions.requirement_conflict: amend design/data to remove conflict; if a business trade-off is required -> escalate:product_owner
resolutions.infeasible: descope, or escalate:product_owner for a scope/priority decision

ladder.note: Final authority. Only the Product Owner can change scope/business requirements. The decision is recorded and the issue is re-dispatched against the corrected spec (or closed).
